name: Nazareth
description: Nazareth, a town in the Galilee region of northern Israel, holds immense significance in Christian mythology and tradition as the childhood home of Jesus. It is where Jesus spent his formative years, growing in wisdom and stature before embarking on his public ministry. The Gospels depict Nazareth as a humble and relatively insignificant village, highlighting the unexpected nature of God's choice to raise his son in such a place. The Annunciation, where the angel Gabriel appeared to Mary and announced that she would conceive and bear the Son of God, is traditionally believed to have occurred in Nazareth. This event marks Nazareth as a place of divine intervention and the beginning of Jesus's earthly journey. While the Hebrew Bible does not mention Nazareth, its importance in the New Testament firmly establishes it as a sacred site for Christians worldwide. The inhabitants of Nazareth initially struggled to accept Jesus as the Messiah, questioning his origins and familiarity. This rejection underscores the theme of faith and the challenge of recognizing divinity in the ordinary. Nazareth thus represents both the humble beginnings of Jesus and the initial resistance to his message, making it a crucial location for understanding the narrative of Christianity.
subtitle: Jesus' childhood home; Annunciation site

keyMyths.title: The Annunciation
keyMyths.description: The angel Gabriel appears to Mary in Nazareth and tells her that she will conceive and give birth to Jesus, the Son of God. This event marks the beginning of Jesus's earthly journey and highlights Nazareth as a place of divine intervention.

keyMyths.title: Rejection at Nazareth
keyMyths.description: When Jesus returns to Nazareth and preaches in the synagogue, the people of Nazareth are skeptical and reject him, questioning his authority and origins. This rejection underscores the theme of faith and the difficulty of recognizing divinity in the familiar.

sources.author: Luke the Evangelist
sources.description: Provides accounts of the Annunciation and Jesus's early life in Nazareth.
sources.text: Provides accounts of the Annunciation and Jesus's early life in Nazareth.

sources.author: Matthew the Evangelist
sources.description: Mentions Nazareth as Jesus's home and fulfills prophecy.
sources.text: Mentions Nazareth as Jesus's home and fulfills prophecy.
